text.title: Build-in-Public Content Engine Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Build-in-Public Content Engine methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each week captures four inputs: what shipped / what broke / what learned / one number. Missing inputs = no engine.
rule.rationale: Each week captures four inputs: what shipped / what broke / what learned / one number. Missing inputs = no engine.

rule.statement: Engine outputs ONLY the 5 standard formats: build-log, learning, screenshot/demo, number-of-the-week, ask. New formats reset metric baseline.
rule.rationale: Engine outputs ONLY the 5 standard formats: build-log, learning, screenshot/demo, number-of-the-week, ask. New formats reset metric baseline.

rule.statement: Every post writes ≥3 hook variants; one ships. Single-hook posts forbidden — first hook is rarely best.
rule.rationale: Every post writes ≥3 hook variants; one ships. Single-hook posts forbidden — first hook is rarely best.

rule.statement: Long-form (IH / newsletter) is source; X / LinkedIn atomize from it. Reverse direction (X → long-form) forbidden as primary flow.
rule.rationale: Long-form (IH / newsletter) is source; X / LinkedIn atomize from it. Reverse direction (X → long-form) forbidden as primary flow.

rule.statement: Monthly review reports followers / list / inbound. If any metric is flat for 6 weeks, engine reconfigures (channel mix / format mix).
rule.rationale: Monthly review reports followers / list / inbound. If any metric is flat for 6 weeks, engine reconfigures (channel mix / format mix).

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-build-in-public-content-engine.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
